item.title: US and Iran: the art of the possible
item.description: Stopgap agreement leaves key issues to be decided in later talks

item.title: BP is deeply dysfunctional, but it could be worse
item.description: Investors should be concerned by Manifold’s ousting, but better to fire too many executives than too few

item.title: The steroid Olympics fell short of its own finishing line
item.description: Doping alone will not guarantee triumph in sport

item.title: Today’s windfall is a warning for state oil companies
item.description: Continuing to invest as if yesterday’s assumptions will govern tomorrow’s world means they won’t be around in 40 years

item.title: Putin and Trump don’t have the cards
item.description: The wars Russia and America have waged in Ukraine and Iran are case studies in geopolitical self-harm

item.title: Stocks’ unshakeable optimism
item.description: The bright side has been the only place to be

item.title: SpaceX to drive a Cybertruck through corporate governance norms
item.description: Provisions in the IPO prospectus will give Elon Musk more freedom — and potentially less accountability

item.title: AI stories aren’t inevitably ‘not art’
item.description: Exercising our own judgment when it comes to quality is something we should not outsource to machines

item.title: Three ways a new Labour leader could change the politics of growth
item.description: The UK needs to confront the policies that have made us poorer

item.title: There are more important things to consider when moving countries than tax
item.description: Relocating to a low-tax jurisdiction often involves unforeseen legal, emotional and social costs

item.title: The predatory advance of surveillance pricing
item.description: Extracting the maximum possible surplus by using personalised data should not be legal

item.title: The UK’s best-performing bank is probably not the one you think
item.description: Shares in London-listed but Georgia-based Lion Finance have risen 800% since 2021

item.title: The energy shock is not over yet
item.description: Even with a deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, supplies will take time to normalise

item.title: The 19th-century guide to running an effective meeting
item.description: A US officer and engineer devised the process that is still in use today

item.title: Counting the cost of New York’s sky-high food bills
item.description: Spiralling prices could lead to more public programmes

item.title: Iran is beating Trump at the art of the deal
item.description: The war could end with Tehran more confident, more hardline and with new resources to rebuild its nuclear programme

item.title: Prize draw businesses have better luck than the average punter
item.description: Lack of regulation has helped draw operators to thrive

item.title: The infantilism of an ‘ungovernable’ Britain
item.description: Political stability is needed if the country is ever to solve its problems

item.title: Investors can no longer rely on a ‘policy put’
item.description: The capacity of policymakers to respond to shocks is down across the board

item.title: It’s not just SpaceX: Big Tech is dominating bond markets too
item.description: US tech giants are tapping markets as they race to build AI data centres

item.title: Why do architects hate washing lines so much?
item.description: Visible laundry is often shunned as unsightly clutter — and increasingly banned. Hanging it out has become a political act

item.title: Tech giants need oversight to protect national security
item.description: Companies such as Anthropic and SpaceX need a presidentially-nominated, Senate-confirmed director on their boards

item.title: Robotaxis need to be tested in real traffic
item.description: To achieve safe, cost-effective autonomy we need to see how other road users react to the vehicles

item.title: Just stop moaning and pay your taxes
item.description: Fetishising tax avoidance in the US must end if the republic is to survive

item.title: Why IPO mania could signal top of the market
item.description: A potential deluge of AI-related equity supply removes a source of upthrust for prices
